combined_cases.ai_summary: Single mother of two children served eviction notice due to late rent, seeking help with rent and bills in desperate need. Imminent eviction with children elevates to P2 priority. Caseworker has made contact and case is at NM Approval stage.
Client seeking assistance to settle outstanding debt of late husband. Long-term debt settlement matter not requiring immediate intervention. Caseworker has made contact and assessment is in progress.

Client in crisis accommodation due to domestic violence with ongoing health issues requiring surgery and housing assistance. Discharged from hospital and needs accommodation to recover. No caseworker interaction recorded.

Client experienced family violence and has been moving house to house, currently at property where uncle left over a month ago without awareness. Housing crisis following family violence requires immediate intervention. No caseworker interaction recorded.
Single mother abandoned by husband following family violence incident. Historical DV without current safety threat, general single parent hardship. No caseworker interaction recorded.
Single mother with 9-month-old son in rent arrears close to eviction, unable to afford food, petrol, and bills. Imminent housing loss with infant elevates to P2. No caseworker interaction recorded.

Client with mental health issues unable to find employment to support family financially, seeking assistance for basic needs. General hardship without immediate crisis. No caseworker interaction recorded.
Client requesting zakat assistance to clear rental arrears. Standard rent assistance request without immediate eviction threat or timeline specified. No caseworker interaction recorded.

Client with outstanding health issues struggling to pay for medication, rent, energy bills, transport, and food. General hardship with health complications but no immediate crisis. No caseworker interaction recorded.

Client with health issues on Centrelink income struggling with rent, energy bills, medication, and transport costs. General hardship without immediate crisis indicators. No caseworker interaction recorded.
Client seeking medical support for chronic anaemia, unable to wait for public health system. Medical need but not emergency level requiring immediate intervention. No caseworker interaction recorded.

Single mother and refugee surviving domestic violence caring for four young children including toddler under extremely difficult circumstances. Complex family situation requiring support but no immediate crisis specified. No caseworker interaction recorded.

Nursing student whose husband's work hours have been significantly reduced affecting their financial situation. General financial hardship without immediate crisis indicators. No caseworker interaction recorded.

Client lost job in February 2026 with existing lending obligations, unable to find new employment. General financial hardship following job loss without immediate crisis. No caseworker interaction recorded.

Client arrived in 2023, married and applied for spouse visa, currently in fourth year in Australia seeking assistance. General support need for recent arrival without immediate crisis. No caseworker interaction recorded.

Client with heart problems and spinal/neck disc issues preventing work, struggling with daily living expenses including specialists and medication. Health-related hardship without immediate crisis. No caseworker interaction recorded.
Domestic violence victim seeking financial support and counseling, referred by psychologist. Historical DV without current safety threat, general support need. No caseworker interaction recorded.

Married couple on student visa with 6-month-old baby with Triple X syndrome, experiencing financial hardship following unplanned birth. General hardship without immediate crisis. No caseworker interaction recorded.

Client was working in warehouse but was terminated, has family and is on protection visa 866, Centrelink will not provide payments, starting new job next week but has current difficulties. General hardship with near-term employment solution. No caseworker interaction recorded.

Parent had to leave job two months ago due to son's ongoing health conditions requiring frequent hospitalization, unable to work and not receiving income support. General hardship following employment loss for family care. No caseworker interaction recorded.

Client needs two weeks advance rent and washing machine/dryer, previously homeless with son but now housed. Standard housing support needs without immediate crisis. No caseworker interaction recorded.

Third party application for emergency relief for recently arrived refugee single mother with four children adjusting to life in Australia. General support for new arrival family without immediate crisis. No caseworker interaction recorded.

Family with six children facing serious financial hardship with only husband working and very high expenses. General family hardship without immediate crisis indicators. No caseworker interaction recorded.
Client requesting food voucher and fuel voucher assistance. Basic needs support request without immediate crisis indicators. No caseworker interaction recorded.

Case manager assisting single mother with three children (ages 25-21-16) who is also caring for elderly mother. Complex family support situation without immediate crisis. No caseworker interaction recorded.

ai_analysis.quality_summary: Significant priority assignment issues identified. Multiple cases with clear urgency indicators (eviction notices, homelessness, domestic violence with housing crisis) remain unassigned or incorrectly prioritized. Most cases lack caseworker interaction despite containing urgent needs.

flags.reason: Single mother served eviction notice should be P2 for imminent eviction, not P3
Family violence with housing crisis and homelessness requires immediate P1 response
Crisis accommodation due to domestic violence with health issues needs P1 priority
Rent arrears close to eviction with infant requires P2 for imminent housing loss
Single mother in desperate need should have priority assignment for proper workflow
Single mother with four children in difficult circumstances needs priority assignment
Recently arrived refugee single mother with four children needs priority assignment
Debt settlement for deceased husband is long-term matter, not imminent crisis
Previously homeless client now housed but needing assistance should have priority
Parent unable to work due to child's health condition needs priority assignment

patterns: Most cases remain unassigned despite clear need descriptions
Several urgent housing/domestic violence cases lack appropriate prioritization
Minimal caseworker interaction across all cases
Generic program participation requests mixed with genuine crisis situations
